meta_description.text: Customize your npmx.dev experience with theme, language, and display preferences.

description.text: Choose where npmx gets search data. Individual package pages always use the npm registry directly.

npm_description.text: Fetches search, org and user listings directly from the official npm registry. Authoritative, but can be slower.

algolia_description.text: Uses Algolia for faster search, org and user pages.

include_types_description.text: Add {'@'}types package to install commands for untyped packages

hide_platform_packages_description.text: Hide native binary packages like {'@'}esbuild/linux-x64 from results

contribute_hint.text: Help improve this translation by adding the missing keys.

vanity_downloads_hint.text: Vanity number: no packages displayed | Vanity number: for the displayed package | Vanity number: Sum of {count} displayed packages

not_found_message.text: The package could not be found.

title_deps.text: Significant dependency count increase since v{version}

title_both.text: Significant size and dependency increase since v{version}

title.text: You might not need this dependency.

native.text: This can be replaced with {replacement}, available since Node {nodeVersion}.

simple.text: The {community} has flagged this package as redundant, with the advice: {replacement}.

documented.text: The {community} has flagged this package as having more performant alternatives.

none.text: This package has been flagged as no longer needed, and its functionality is likely available natively in all engines.

total.text: {size} total unpacked size (including {count} dependency for linux-x64) | {size} total unpacked size (including all {count} dependencies for linux-x64)

not_available_detail.text: We could not generate docs for this version.

dev_dependency_hint.text: Usually installed as a dev dependency

view.text: {packageName} has the same maintainer. Click for more details.

fallback_install_provenance.text: Install commands are pinned to {version}, the last version with provenance.

fallback_install_trustedPublisher.text: Install commands are pinned to {version}, the last version with trusted publishing.

all_covered.text: All versions are covered by tags above

recent_versions_only_tooltip.text: Show only versions published within the last year.

show_low_usage_tooltip.text: Include version groups with less than 1% of total downloads.

filter_tooltip.text: Filter versions using a {link}. For example, ^3.0.0 shows all 3.x versions.

general_description.text: Bar chart showing per-version downloads for {versions_count} {semver_grouping_mode} versions of the {package_name} package, {date_range_label} from the {first_version} version to the {last_version} version. The most downloaded version is {max_downloaded_version} with {max_version_downloads} downloads. {per_version_analysis}. {watermark}.

outdated_major.text: {count} major version behind (latest: {latest}) | {count} major versions behind (latest: {latest})

outdated_minor.text: {count} minor version behind (latest: {latest}) | {count} minor versions behind (latest: {latest})

show_all.text: show {count} peer dep | show all {count} peer deps
show {count} optional dep | show all {count} optional deps

contributors_skip.text: Not shown in Contributors (no GitHub repo):

known_anomalies_description.text: Interpolates over known download spikes caused by bots or CI issues.

known_anomalies_none.text: No known anomalies for this package. | No known anomalies for these packages.

watermark.text: At the bottom, a watermark reads "./npmx a fast, modern browser for the npm registry"

analysis.text: {package_name} starts at {start_value} and ends at {end_value}, showing a {trend} trend with a slope of {downloads_slope} downloads per time interval

estimation.text: The final value is an estimate based on partial data for the current period.

estimations.text: The final values are estimates based on partial data for the current period.

compare.text: Package download comparison line chart for: {packages}.

single_package.text: Downloads line chart for the {package} package.

general_description.text: The Y axis represents the number of downloads. The X axis represents the date range, from {start_date} to {end_date}, with a {granularity} time period.{estimation_notice} {packages_analysis}. {watermark}.

tree_found.text: {vulns} vulnerability in {packages}/{total} packages | {vulns} vulnerabilities in {packages}/{total} packages

show_all_packages.text: show {count} affected package | show all {count} affected packages

packages_failed.text: {count} package could not be checked | {count} packages could not be checked

show_all.text: show {count} deprecated package | show all {count} deprecated packages

connected_hint.text: You can now manage packages and organizations from the web UI.

paste_token.text: Then paste the token below to connect:

warning_text.text: This allows npmx to access your npm CLI. Only connect to sites you trust.

empty_hint.text: Add operations from package or org pages

no_packages_hint.text: This organization may not exist or has no public packages.

own_orgs_only.text: You can only view your own organizations.

empty_hint.text: Organizations are detected from your scoped packages.

success_hint.text: You can now publish new versions to this package using npm publish.

missing_permission.text: You do not have permission to add a package to scope {'@'}{scope}.

similar_warning.text: Similar packages exist - npm may reject this name:

scope_warning_title.text: Consider using a scoped package instead

scope_warning_text.text: Unscoped package names are a shared resource. Only claim a name if you intend to publish and maintain a package. For personal or organizational projects, use a scoped name like {'@'}{username}/{name}.

connect_required.text: Connect to the local connector to claim this package name.

publish_hint.text: This will publish a minimal placeholder package.

file_size_warning.text: {size} exceeds the 500KB limit for syntax highlighting

unavailable_hint.text: The file may be too large or unavailable

version_required.text: Version is required to browse code

search_placeholder_all.text: Search all or use name: desc: kw:

scope_all_description.text: Search all fields, supports name: desc: kw: operators

meta_description.text: npmx is a fast, modern browser for the npm registry. A better UX/DX for exploring npm packages.

description.text: npmx is a {betterUxDx} for the npm package registry and tooling. We provide a fast, modern interface for exploring packages, with features like dark mode, keyboard navigation, code browsing, and connections to alternative registries like {jsr}.

admin_description.text: We also aim to provide a better {adminUi} for managing your packages, teams, and organizations — all from the browser, powered by your local npm CLI.

registry_description.text: We don't host packages. We're just a better way to browse them.

title.text: ... and {count} more contributor | ... and {count} more contributors

description.text: npmx is fully open source, built by an amazing community of contributors. Join us and let's build the npm browsing experience we always wanted, together.
Help us build a better npm experience.
Chat, ask questions, and share ideas.
Find out the latest on npmx.

atmosphere_explanation.text: {npmx} uses the {atproto} to power many of its social features, allowing users to own their data and use one account for all compatible applications. Once you create an account, you can use other apps like {bluesky} and {tangled} with the same account.

default_input_error.text: Please enter a valid handle, DID, or a full PDS URL

tagline.text: compare npm packages side-by-side to help you choose the right one.

error.text: Failed to load package data. Please try again.

empty_description.text: Search and add at least 2 packages above to see a side-by-side comparison of their metrics.

add_hint.text: Add at least 2 packages to compare.

typeahead_description.text: Compare against not using a dependency! e18e approved.

tooltip_title.text: You might not need a dependency

tooltip_description.text: Compare against not using a dependency! The {link} maintains a list of packages that can be replaced with native APIs or simpler alternatives.

binary_only_tooltip.text: This package exposes binaries and no exports

description.text: Total install size including all dependencies
Total number of dependencies including transitive

select_file_prompt.text: Select a file from the sidebar to view its diff

welcome.text: Welcome to {app}. We are committed to protecting your privacy. This policy explains what data we collect, how we use it, and your rights regarding your information.

p1.text: Cookies are small text files stored on your device when you visit a website. Their purpose is to enhance your browsing experience by remembering certain preferences and settings.
We only use {bold} for purposes strictly necessary for the site's functionality. We do not use third-party or advertising cookies.

cookie_vdpl_desc.text: This cookie is used by our hosting provider (Vercel) for skew protection. It ensures you fetch assets from the correct deployment version if a new update is released while you are browsing. It does not track you.

cookie_h3_desc.text: This is our secure session cookie. It stores the OAuth access token when you connect your Atmosphere account. It is essential for maintaining your authenticated session.

p1.text: In addition to session cookies, we use your browser's {bold} to save your display preferences. This allows us to remember the theme (light/dark) and some other {settings} you have selected, so you don't have to reconfigure them on each visit.

p2.text: This information is purely functional, stored only on your device, and {bold2}. We use it exclusively to improve your experience on our website.

bold2.text: contains no personal data nor is it used to track you

p1.text: You can configure your browser to accept, reject, or delete cookies according to your preferences. However, please note that {bold}.

bold.text: rejecting essential cookies may prevent full access to the application

p2.text: Below are links with instructions for cookie management in the most commonly used browsers:

chrome.text: Google Chrome (opens in a new window)

firefox.text: Mozilla Firefox (opens in a new window)

edge.text: Microsoft Edge (opens in a new window)

p1.text: We use {bold} to understand how visitors use our website. This helps us improve the user experience and identify issues.

p2.text: Vercel Analytics is designed with privacy in mind:

li2.text: It does not collect personal identifiers

li3.text: It does not track users across websites

li4.text: All data is aggregated and anonymised

p3.text: The only information collected includes: page URLs, referrer, country/region, device type, browser, and operating system. This data cannot be used to identify individual users.

p1.text: When you connect your {bold} account to npmx, we store your OAuth access token in a secure, HTTP-only session cookie. This token is used solely to authenticate requests on your behalf.

p2.text: We do not store your credentials, and we do not access any data beyond what is necessary to provide the features you use. You can disconnect your account at any time from the {settings} page.

p1.text: Session cookies are automatically deleted when you close your browser or after a period of inactivity. Local storage preferences remain on your device until you clear your browser data. Analytics data is retained in aggregate form and cannot be linked to individual users.

li1.text: Access information about what data we collect

li2.text: Clear your local storage and cookies at any time

li4.text: Request information about our data practices

p2.text: Since we do not collect personal data, there is typically no personal information to delete or export.

p1.text: For any questions or concerns about this privacy policy, you can contact us by opening an issue on our {link}.
We may update this privacy policy from time to time. Any changes will be published on this page with an updated revision date.

welcome.text: We want {app} to be usable by as many people as possible.

p1.text: We try to follow the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.2 and use them as a reference when building features. We don't claim full conformance with any level of WCAG — accessibility is a continual process and there is always more work to do.

p2.text: This site is an {about}. Accessibility improvements are made incrementally as part of our regular development.

p1.text: Some of the things we aim to do across the site:

li1.text: Use semantic HTML and ARIA attributes where appropriate.

li2.text: Use relative text sizes so you can adjust them in your browser.

li3.text: Support keyboard navigation throughout the interface.

li4.text: Respect the prefers-reduced-motion and prefers-color-scheme media queries.

li5.text: Design with sufficient color contrast in mind.

li6.text: Ensure essential content is available without JavaScript, though some interactive features require it.

p1.text: Some parts of the site — particularly third-party content like package READMEs — may not meet accessibility standards. We are working to improve these areas over time.
If you encounter an accessibility barrier on {app}, please let us know by opening an issue on our {link}. We take these reports seriously and will do our best to address them.

meta_description.text: The npmx team was recharging. Discord reopened after a week.

subtitle.text: we were building npmx at a pace that was costing {some} of us sleep. we didn't want that to be the norm! so we took a week off. together.

illustration_alt.text: a single row of cosy icons

p2.text: all invite links were gone and channels were locked – except {garden}, which stayed open for folks who wanted to keep hanging out.

p1.text: {site} and {repo} stayed open – people still dug in, filed some issues, opened a few PRs, but mainly everyone spent time somewhere near a cosy fireplace.
we came back recharged and ready for the final push to March 3rd. {social} for updates.
